title: Neoporteria occulta
taxonomy: Neoporteria occulta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Opuntioideae, which is a group of cacti that produce flat, round stems. Neoporteria occulta is further classified into the genus Neoporteria, which includes several other species of cacti. The plant is native to South America, specifically in the regions of Chile and Argentina. Neoporteria occulta is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and overcollection.

intro: Neoporteria occulta, also known as Eriosyce occulta, is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Neoporteria, characterized by its small size and unique, globular shape. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria occulta include flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
